decision.archive_summary: The conversation iterated on adding Codex minor GC regression coverage: first a repeatable smoke/eval case for GC-D2, then harder Chinese multi-topic detour/return cases, and finally tightening the guard logic so passing behavior was not achieved via brittle allowlisting. The user explicitly objected to whitelist-style hardcoding and wanted the fix to generalize. A previously failing test was expected to pass after these changes, and the final action was to run the available automated live eval script, which the assistant reported had been run.

Earlier turns describe completed implementation steps and resolved expectations; they are no longer needed as raw prompt context for the next turn. The only durable preference worth preserving semantically is t7: avoid whitelist-like hardcoding and prefer a general solution. Because the latest user turn is retained by the runtime automatically, it is not listed for eviction.
